host_spec.host_caveat: dev host where source tree lives; install paths confined to /tmp scratch dir; no source-tree dev install used; registries only. The 'fresh host' constraint in 0.6.1-V.md is 'ideally' (soft); this run satisfies the hard constraints (no CI, no source-tree dev install, no release-notes edits).

per_binding_smoke_verdict.python: GREEN — Engine.open, write, search, close, admin.configure all returned expected shapes
per_binding_smoke_verdict.typescript: GREEN — Engine.open, write, search, close, admin.configure all returned expected shapes
per_binding_smoke_verdict.rust: GREEN — Engine::open (via OpenedEngine destructure), write (Node + AdminSchema kind=latest_state), search, close all OK

notes_for_orchestrator: All three published axis-W artifacts at 0.6.1-rc.1 install and exercise the 5-verb surface end-to-end.
OpenReport accessor (12-TX-OPENREPORT closure / 0.6.1 anchor) verified populated and idempotent in py + ts; rust reads it via the OpenedEngine.report field at open-time as designed.

Smoke initially tripped on the py binding because the docs/release-notes show the OpenReport snippets but not the admin.configure call; correct signature is fathomdb.admin.configure(engine, name=..., body=...) (top-level module). Body '{}' is accepted. Documented for any future V-slice rerun.

Rust direct PreparedWrite::AdminSchema requires kind ∈ {latest_state, append_only_log} (per fathomdb-py admin_configure sugar at lib.rs:495); plain 'doc' kind is rejected with SchemaValidation. Not a binding bug — Rust is the raw substrate, py/ts hide the kind constant.
